title: Reboot of your system might kill your container image files
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I am using a clean F31 distribution. On my server i created a technical user (id > 1000, not the first) , that is dedicated to run a podman-container, rootless of course. I got my server into run state and before a shutdown i make sure, that the container is stopped by podman stop <container id>.

After reboot i want to restart the podman-container, but the container-file is not there. The when i try to remove that image, podman tells me, that there is no file.

```
[tlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                       COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS                     PORTS                        NAMES
a6edeae3c880  localhost/kivi_prod:latest  /lib/systemd/syst...  42 minutes ago  Exited (0) 30 minutes ago  192.168.178.39:9190->80/tcp  jovial_stonebraker
[tlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ podman start a6edeae3c880
Error: unable to start container "a6edeae3c880": cannot chown run directory /run/user/1001/overlay-containers/a6edeae3c8807b9d39b54733566c2533dd53f8efbcd92365b98913896261bb39/userdata: chown /run/user/1001/overlay-containers/a6edeae3c8807b9d39b54733566c2533dd53f8efbcd92365b98913896261bb39/userdata: no such file or directory
[tlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ podman rm a6edeae3c880
Error: error freeing lock for container a6edeae3c8807b9d39b54733566c2533dd53f8efbcd92365b98913896261bb39: no such file or directory
[tlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ podman rm a6edeae3c880
Error: no container with name or ID a6edeae3c880 found: no such container
```
gaaahhh!


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. get a user with a uid > 1000. It looks like, that the very first user does not have this problem

2. stop your container

3. reboot und try to restart the container again

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[tlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ podman start a6edeae3c880
Error: unable to start container "a6edeae3c880": cannot chown run directory /run/user/1001/overlay-containers/a6edeae3c8807b9d39b54733566c2533dd53f8efbcd92365b98913896261bb39/userdata: chown /run/user/1001/overlay-containers/a6edeae3c8807b9d39b54733566c2533dd53f8efbcd92365b98913896261bb39/userdata: no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
a proper start!

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[tlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ podman version
Version:            1.7.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.5
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[tlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.13.5
  podman version: 1.7.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.12.0
  CgroupVersion: v2
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.10-2.fc31.x86_64

    version: 'conmon version 2.0.10, commit: 6b526d9888abb86b9e7de7dfdeec0da98ad32ee0'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "31"
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1001
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 165536
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 15688372224
  MemTotal: 16427331584
  OCIRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.10.6-1.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.10.6
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 8300523520
  SwapTotal: 8300523520
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: chasmash
  kernel: 5.4.15-200.fc31.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    Executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    Package: slirp4netns-0.4.0-20.1.dev.gitbbd6f25.fc31.x86_64
    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.3+dev
      commit: bbd6f25c70d5db2a1cd3bfb0416a8db99a75ed7e
  uptime: 44m 9.47s
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
  - quay.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/tlxoffice/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: fuse-overlayfs-0.7.5-2.fc31.x86_64
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.6.2
        fuse-overlayfs: version 0.7.5
        FUSE library version 3.6.2
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29
  GraphRoot: /mnt/raidSpace/Homes/tlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 77
  RunRoot: /run/user/1001
  VolumePath: /mnt/raidSpace/Homes/tlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[tlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ rpm -q -f /usr/bin/podman
podman-1.7.0-2.fc31.x86_64

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
a proper Fedora F31

comments.body:
No such file or directory on rootless storage directory? We should be recreating that on detecting a reboot. RunRoot is set appropriately to a tmpfs, so it looks as though we ought to be wiping the alive file after reboot, which is confusing. 

`Error: error freeing lock for container a6edeae3c8807b9d39b54733566c2533dd53f8efbcd92365b98913896261bb39: no such file or directory`
That is not particularly concerning - looks like another consequence of the refresh code not running after a reboot. The question becomes whether the code is running, but erroring out, or if it is not running at all. A debug trace (add `--log-level=debug` to a Podman command) of the first Podman process run after a reboot would be appreciated.

Sorry, 
i am a bit confused, because i would never put a container image on a tempoary filesystem.
The curious thing is, that this does not happend on a workstation F31 Installation with a user having a uid 1000. 
I want to point our,that is stopped the container before i initiate a reboot. So there should not be any volatile data on a tmpfs directory at all, am i right.-

- How can i support to solve it ?
- How can i make sure, that the stopped state of the container will not get lost ?

What is happening is that Podman is not detecting and appropriately handling the reboot; as such locks are not reallocated, temporary directories are not recreated, etc. We detect reboots via a file created on a tmpfs directory, somewhere in `RunRoot` (as seen in `podman info`), which appears to be a tmpfs filesystem and should be recreated after reboot. If you can provide a `--log-level=debug` trace of the first Podman command run after a reboot, it would help debug what is going on (whether we are not detecting the reboot at all, or encountering an error when we try to handle it).

Salut Matthew,
first of all, thank you for taking some time to help me!
ok, here is what you have asked me for:

Smaller update: /run/user/1001/overlay-containers is empty.
Please let me know, if you have a workaround for this behaviour.

`DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /var/tmp/run-1001/libpod/tmp   `

Is this directory on a tmpfs?

If not, that is why we're not detecting reboot. You could try deleting the libpod database at `/mnt/raidSpace/Homes/tlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db` and your containers.conf (usually lives in `~/.config/containers/libpod.conf`) and see if tmp dir is moved into `/run/user/1001` where it should be.

Salut Matthew,

the directory `/var/tmp/run-1001/libpod/tmp` was not a tmpfs. So i removed the user and created a new technical user. After a reboot, the very first podman call gave me this:

```
 - - - -
INFO[0000] running as rootless                          
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"              
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /mnt/raidSpace/Homes/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /mnt/raidSpace/Homes/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /run/user/1001/containers     
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /mnt/raidSpace/Homes/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1001/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /mnt/raidSpace/Homes/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] No store required. Not opening container store. 
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend journald          
DEBU[0000] using runtime "/usr/bin/runc"                
- - - -
```
So i figured out, where he goes:
```
[techlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ df -k /run/user/1001/libpod/tmp
Dateisystem    1K-Blöcke Benutzt Verfügbar Verw% Eingehängt auf
tmpfs            1604232       4   1604228    1% /run/user/1001
```
looks better. So let's start the stopped container:

```
[techlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                       COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS                     PORTS                        NAMES
29dc6c4e0d90  localhost/kivi_prod:latest  /lib/systemd/syst...  39 minutes ago  Exited (0) 12 minutes ago  192.168.178.39:9190->80/tcp  boring_zhukovsky
[techlxoffice@chasmash docker_kivitendo]$ podman start 29dc6c4e0d90
29dc6c4e0d9038ae7298b8faef2002d76476032c5aeb1002fd838adb3f702140
```
Isn't this fantastic!

Thanks Matthew!
